name: Warsaw Federal Savings And Loan Association
description_short: Community-focused mutual bank serving the greater Cincinnati area since 1893, offering checking, savings, mortgages, and business loans with a commitment to underserved communities.
description_long:
Warsaw Federal Savings and Loan Association was founded in 1893 in Cincinnati's Price Hill neighborhood to provide financial services to residents and businesses. The bank has maintained its community-banking mission for 130 years, recently designating itself as a Minority Depository Institution (MDI) to formalize its commitment to serving historically underserved communities in the greater Cincinnati area. As a mutual bank structure, the institution is owned by its depositors rather than external shareholders, which shapes its operational philosophy and reinvestment priorities.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of retail and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), mortgage loans, business loans, and a mobile banking platform. Current CD rates advertised include 3.55% APY for 7-month terms, 3.29% for 13-month terms, and 3.29% for 25-month terms. Warsaw Federal provides a Closing Cost Assistance Program offering up to $2,500 in credits for homebuyers. The institution operates with routing number 242071855 and NMLS number 504911, maintaining both online and in-person banking channels with a mobile app available on iOS and Android.

What distinguishes Warsaw Federal is its explicit positioning as a mutual bank that reinvests deposits directly into the local community through mortgage and business lending, charitable donations, and community support initiatives. The bank emphasizes its MDI status as central to its identity, focusing on economic empowerment in historically underserved neighborhoods. Management highlights that "everyone can bank here" and positions itself as a values-driven institution where community impact is a stated operational priority alongside traditional banking functions.

The main caveat is that Warsaw Federal's service area appears geographically limited to the greater Cincinnati region, which may restrict access for non-local customers. While the website emphasizes community commitment and fair lending practices, typical banking limitations apply—mortgage approval depends on creditworthiness, and business loan availability likely requires standard underwriting. The bank's mutual structure and MDI designation are genuine differentiators, though these primarily affect lending philosophy rather than account holder benefits or rates relative to other regional banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with competitive rates
Mortgage loans and home purchase financing
Closing Cost Assistance Program (up to $2,500 credits)
Personal online banking and account management
Credit management tools and credit score insights
pros: Mutual bank structure owned by depositors, not shareholders, with 130+ year history of community reinvestment
Minority Depository Institution (MDI) designation demonstrates formal commitment to underserved communities and economic empowerment
Closing Cost Assistance Program offers up to $2,500 in closing cost credits for eligible homebuyers
Competitive CD rates (3.55% for 7-month, 3.29% for 13/25-month terms) higher than national averages
Mobile banking app provides secure account access and credit management tools integrated into the platform
Dual enrollment options for personal and business online banking, serving both individual and commercial customers
Community-focused mission explicitly prioritizes local economic development alongside shareholder/depositor returns
cons: Service area appears limited to greater Cincinnati region, restricting accessibility for customers outside the area
Limited information on website about interest rates for checking/savings accounts or account fee structures
No mention of credit card products, though full product range not completely documented on available pages
As a smaller regional bank, likely has fewer branch locations and less brand recognition than national competitors
Website lacks transparency about mortgage approval criteria, business loan requirements, or minimum account balance requirements
best_for: Cincinnati-area residents seeking community-focused banking with local reinvestment impact
First-time homebuyers in the greater Cincinnati region who qualify for the $2,500 closing cost assistance
Small business owners in underserved communities looking for lenders committed to economic development
Depositors who prioritize supporting mutual bank structures and local community growth over corporate shareholder returns
diagnosis: Warsaw Federal is best for greater Cincinnati-area residents and small business owners who prioritize community impact and want their deposits reinvested locally. The main caveat is geographic limitation—if you're outside the Cincinnati region or need national banking convenience, you'll likely need a larger institution, though the mutual bank structure and MDI commitment make it an excellent choice for qualified local borrowers seeking ethical, community-focused banking.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
